narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY down 0.33% with USD scoring zero on H4 CSM while Fed hawkish pushback fails to arrest dollar selling pressure
2. CHF and EUR acceleration because H4 CSM shows CHF at 100 and EUR at 99 diverging sharply upward from flat D1 readings, driving USDCHF to strong sell signals
3. JPY cross dynamics because suspected BoJ intervention combined with JPY sitting weakest on H4 CSM at 40 creates directional tension in all JPY crosses as equities rise

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD H4 strong buy at plus five and USDCHF H4 strong sell at minus five are the clearest confirmation that this is a USD selling event rather than idiosyncratic pair moves, validated by the 0.86 EURUSD-GBPUSD correlation moving in lockstep. AUDUSD and NZDUSD both registering strong buy at plus four with a 0.93 mutual correlation confirm the anti-dollar theme is broad. The JPY cross sells on H4 despite USDJPY correlation to EURJPY at 0.94 suggest the yen is being dragged by equity strength rather than intervention holding, meaning JPY cross direction depends entirely on whether equities sustain gains.

REGIME

Mixed confirmed leaning risk-on: VIX down 2.01% to 16.55 supports the read, S&P 500 up 0.55% to 7249 adds confirmation, but Gold up 0.92% to 4657 alongside silver up 4.65% introduces a safe haven undercurrent that prevents a clean risk-on classification and points to ongoing uncertainty about the macro trajectory.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on regime: Gold and silver surging sharply contradicts the VIX compression and equity rally, suggesting the market is simultaneously buying protection and risk assets which undermines a pure directional read
2. CAD CSM fading: CAD scores 91 on D1 CSM but only 52 on H4 CSM while oil is down 4.27%, meaning CAD fundamental strength is deteriorating in real time and USDCAD sell signals may be premature or short-lived

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because CHF scores 100 on H4 CSM with USD at zero and strong sell confirmed on both timeframes - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: GBPUSD up because GBP H4 CSM at 82 with strong buy signal and broad USD selling trend aligning - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: CADJPY because oil down 4.27% undercuts CAD while JPY intervention risk creates two-sided unpredictability
Watch: DXY breaking below 97.50 confirms the USD sell is accelerating and validates both primary and secondary trades simultaneously

themes.theme: Fed hawkish pushback against easing bias
ECB hawkish rhetoric with June hike risk flagged by Nagel and energy price concerns from Makhlouf
Repeated Japan FX intervention suspected to defend JPY
BoE Pill signals prompt action on inflation with active hold and June risks
RBA expected rate hike supporting AUD near recent highs
Higher for longer rates narrative weighing on gold and shaping broader macro outlook
Elevated oil prices with WTI above 102 heading for three year high adding to inflation concerns
US ISM Manufacturing PMI slightly misses at 52.7 vs 53.0 but SP Global PMI beats at 54.5
Iran nuclear negotiation headlines adding geopolitical risk layer
Equity markets rallying to record highs on earnings optimism

key_observation: Central banks globally are in hawkish mode with the Fed explicitly pushing back on easing expectations while the ECB flags June hike risk and the BoE warns of prompt action on inflation. Higher energy prices and sticky inflation are the unifying theme keeping rates higher for longer across G10. USD is caught between supportive rate expectations and softening data with ISM missing estimates while EUR strengthens toward 1.1755. Repeated BOJ intervention in USDJPY underscores JPY vulnerability despite official support.
watch: US ISM follow-through and NFP implications for June Fed pricing. BOJ intervention sustainability and USDJPY 155-160 corridor. ECB June meeting rhetoric as EUR tests 1.1750-1.1800 resistance. WTI trajectory toward three-year highs and second-round inflation effects on central bank reaction functions. Iran negotiation outcome for energy supply risk premium.
